title: Guard Clock page replacement against zero frames infinite loop
description: clockPageReplacement at page-replacement.ts:320 enters while(true) when frameCount=0. hand=(hand+1)%0 yields NaN, refBit[NaN] is undefined, loop never terminates, browser hangs. Add guard: if (frameCount <= 0) return buildResult('Clock', []).
acceptanceCriteria: clockPageReplacement(refs, 0) returns empty result without hanging
All 4 page algorithms guard against frameCount <= 0
Unit test confirms no hang on zero frames

title: Add Round Robin quantum input to CPU scheduling controls
description: OSModule.tsx:2125 hardcodes roundRobin(procs, 2). The engine supports configurable quantum but UI provides no control. Add a number input for quantum (1-20, default 2) visible when RR is selected.
acceptanceCriteria: Number input for quantum appears when Round Robin is selected
Quantum value is passed to roundRobin() engine call
Quantum persists between Run clicks
Compare All uses user-specified quantum for RR

title: Add priority input field to process cards for Priority scheduling
description: OSModule.tsx:2429-2451 shows only Arr and Burst labels. The priority field exists in state but has no input. When Priority or MLFQ is selected, a priority input must be visible.
acceptanceCriteria: Priority input field appears in each process card
Priority value is passed to priorityScheduling()
Priority is visible in the process table in properties panel

title: Expose SJF preemptive toggle and Priority preemptive toggle
description: OSModule.tsx:2121 always calls sjf(procs) non-preemptive. Line 2128 always calls priorityScheduling(procs) non-preemptive. Both engines support preemptive mode. Add checkbox toggles.
acceptanceCriteria: Preemptive checkbox appears when SJF or Priority is selected
SRTF mode produces different results than SJF
Compare All includes both modes

title: Fix orphaned timer when switching away from thread-sync mid-play
description: OSModule.tsx:2083-2104 syncTimerRef continues firing setSyncStep after user switches concept. No useEffect cleanup on active change. Add effect: useEffect(() => { return () => { setSyncPlaying(false); clearInterval(syncTimerRef.current); }; }, [active]).
acceptanceCriteria: Switching concept while thread-sync is playing stops the timer
No console errors from state updates on unmounted concept
Timer restarts correctly when returning to thread-sync

title: Fix duplicate step IDs in bankersAlgorithm requestResources output
description: bankers-algorithm.ts:300 — requestResources calls isSafe which calls resetStepId(), so step IDs restart at 0 mid-trace. The merged steps array has non-monotonic IDs. Fix: pass starting ID as parameter or use closure-scoped counter.
acceptanceCriteria: requestResources output has strictly increasing step IDs
isSafe standalone still works with IDs starting at 0
Unit test asserts monotonic IDs

title: Fix mlfq() in scheduling.ts emitting preempt instead of demote for demotion events
description: scheduling.ts:858 emits type:'preempt' for demotion events. Should be type:'demote' to match mlfq-scheduler.ts:437 and enable correct event log coloring.
acceptanceCriteria: mlfq() demotion events have type:'demote'
Event log colors demotions correctly
Compare All shows correct event types for MLFQ

title: Add ARIA labels, roles, htmlFor/id pairing, and focus indicators to all OS module controls
description: OSModule.tsx has 0 aria-label, 0 role, 0 htmlFor, 0 aria-live attributes across 3933 lines. All inputs use outline-none without custom focus ring. This makes the entire module inaccessible to screen readers and keyboard users.
acceptanceCriteria: Every label has htmlFor matching input id
All icon-only buttons have aria-label
Result areas have aria-live='polite'
All inputs have visible focus ring (focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary)
SVG visualizers have role='img' and descriptive title elements

title: Replace hardcoded hex colors with CSS custom properties across OS module
description: 59 hardcoded hex colors in OSModule.tsx (ALLOC_COLORS, THREAD_COLORS, PROCESS_COLORS at lines 136-165, plus 30+ inline SVG fill values). GCPauseLatencyVisualizer correctly uses var(--primary) etc. Consolidate 3 duplicate color arrays into one OS_PALETTE and use CSS variables for theme compatibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Single OS_PALETTE constant replaces 3 duplicate arrays
SVG fill/stroke values use CSS variables
Colors adapt correctly in dark theme
No visual regression in any visualization

title: Replace sub-11px text with minimum 10px throughout OS module
description: 114 instances of text-[8px] and text-[9px] across OSModule.tsx. Below WCAG minimum readable size. Replace text-[8px] with text-[10px] and text-[9px] with text-[10px] or text-xs. Key locations: Gantt axis (line 208), SVG legends (lines 1090-1094), thread state labels (line 1291), HIT/FAULT badges (line 910).
acceptanceCriteria: No text-[8px] or text-[9px] remains in OS module files
Minimum font size is 10px for data, 11px for labels
Visualizations remain visually balanced after size increase

title: Make Learn tab concept-specific instead of always showing GC visualizer
description: OSModule.tsx:3769-3774 renders GCPauseLatencyVisualizer for ALL 6 concepts. A user studying deadlock sees Java GC content. Each concept needs its own Learn component: scheduling theory for CPU, virtual memory fundamentals for memory, etc.
acceptanceCriteria: Learn tab renders different content based on active concept
Each concept has at minimum a text explainer with key terms defined
GC visualizer only shows for relevant concept or as bonus content
Learn components are lazy-loaded

title: Add input validation and error handling to all simulation run callbacks
description: No try/catch around algorithm calls (OSModule.tsx:2117-2141, 2164-2184, 2228-2244). Invalid inputs silently produce empty results or corrupt data. Negative burst times accepted. Empty process lists produce vacuous results. Add validation before run, try/catch around engines, and error state display.
acceptanceCriteria: Negative burst/arrival values are rejected with inline error
Empty process/reference lists show validation message instead of running
Engine exceptions are caught and displayed as error banners
Frame count 0 is prevented at input level (min=1 enforced)

title: Split OSModule.tsx into 6 concept-specific canvas components
description: OSModule.tsx is 3933 lines with 6 concept branches in one file. Extract each concept canvas into its own file: CPUSchedulingCanvas.tsx, PageReplacementCanvas.tsx, DeadlockCanvas.tsx, MemoryCanvas.tsx, MemAllocCanvas.tsx, ThreadSyncCanvas.tsx. Keep useOSModule hook in a separate useOSModule.ts file.
acceptanceCriteria: Each concept canvas is a separate file under src/components/modules/os/
useOSModule.ts contains only the hook logic
Each canvas component is lazy-loadable
No functional regression in any concept
Main OSModule.tsx is under 200 lines

title: Add step-through mode for CPU scheduling Gantt chart
description: CPU scheduling shows full Gantt instantly (OSModule.tsx:2472-2485) while all other concepts have step controls. Add step-through that highlights one Gantt block at a time, showing the scheduler's decision at each tick with the event description.
acceptanceCriteria: Step/Reset/Show All buttons appear below Gantt chart
Each step highlights the active Gantt block
Event description shows at each step (why this process was chosen)
Properties panel updates per-step metrics

title: Add color legend inside Gantt chart canvas (spatial contiguity)
description: Gantt chart colors are only explained in the Properties panel process table (OSModule.tsx:1526-1558), violating Mayer's spatial contiguity principle. Add an inline process color legend directly below the Gantt chart bars.
acceptanceCriteria: Color legend with process name + color dot appears below Gantt chart
Legend updates when processes are added/removed
Legend is compact (horizontal layout with wrapping)

title: Add pre-training concept explainers at the top of each concept panel
description: No concept defines terms before using them (Mayer's pre-training principle). 'TLB HIT', 'PT HIT', 'TAT', 'Arr' are used without explanation. Add a collapsible 'What is X?' callout at the top of each concept canvas explaining key terms and the visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: Each concept has a collapsible explainer below the title
Key terms (TLB, TAT, quantum, etc.) are defined
Collapsed by default for returning users
Expand state persists in session

title: Clarify Request/Need matrix dual-semantics for Deadlock vs Banker's
description: OSModule.tsx:2760 labels the matrix 'Request / Need Matrix' which has different semantics for Detect Deadlock (requested resources) vs Banker's (remaining need = max - allocated). This is the highest extraneous cognitive load item in the module.
acceptanceCriteria: Matrix label changes based on which algorithm will be run
Tooltip or helper text explains the interpretation
Separate labels: 'Current Requests' for RAG, 'Max Additional Need' for Banker's

title: Add mobile responsive layout with panel collapse and touch targets
description: All action buttons are h-8 (32px), process inputs are h-4 (16px), delete icons are 12x12px — all below 44px touch target minimum. No 3-panel collapse for mobile. PropertiesSheet component exists but is unused. Add: mobile panel drawer, minimum 44px touch targets, safe-area insets for bottom panel.
acceptanceCriteria: All interactive elements meet 44px minimum touch target on mobile
Sidebar collapses to drawer below md breakpoint
Properties panel uses PropertiesSheet on mobile
Bottom panel has safe-area-inset-bottom padding
Deadlock matrix uses tabbed view on mobile instead of side-by-side

title: Add keyboard shortcuts for Run (Ctrl+Enter), Step (→), Reset (Escape)
description: Zero onKeyDown handlers in OSModule.tsx. DatabaseModule has Enter-key handlers on inputs. Add global keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+Enter to Run, → to Step forward, ← to Step back, Escape to Reset. Show shortcut hints on buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+Enter runs the current simulation
Arrow keys advance/retreat steps when step controls are active
Escape resets the current simulation
Keyboard shortcut hints appear on hover/focus of buttons
Enter key on input fields triggers Run

title: Add comprehensive test coverage for untested engines
description: Clock algorithm, all thread-sync primitives, memory simulation, and memory allocation have zero test coverage. Clock has a confirmed while(true) bug. SJF, Priority scheduling also untested. Add unit tests for all 12 engine functions with edge cases.
acceptanceCriteria: clockPageReplacement has tests including 0-frame guard
simulateMutex, simulateSemaphore, simulateReaderWriterLock have basic correctness tests
simulateVirtualMemory has TLB hit/miss/eviction tests
simulateFirstFit/BestFit/WorstFit have fragmentation validation tests
sjf(preemptive=true) and priorityScheduling have correctness tests
Test for requestResources step ID monotonicity

title: Replace module-level mutable counters with closure-scoped state
description: memory-alloc.ts:39 blockIdCounter and bankers-algorithm.ts:69 _stepId are module-level mutable singletons reset on each simulate(). In SSR/concurrent mode, overlapping calls corrupt counters. compareAllocAlgorithms runs 3 simulations resetting counter each time, causing React key collisions.
acceptanceCriteria: blockIdCounter is passed as parameter or uses closure
_stepId is passed as parameter or uses closure
compareAllocAlgorithms produces unique block IDs across all 3 strategies
requestResources produces unique step IDs throughout merged trace

title: Add Random button for generating reference strings and process sets
description: page-replacement.ts:356 exports generateReferenceString() but no UI calls it. Users must manually type comma-separated numbers. Add Random buttons next to reference string input and for generating process sets.
acceptanceCriteria: Random button next to reference string input generates a random string
Random button next to process list generates 3-6 random processes
Generated values use reasonable ranges (pages 0-9, burst 1-10)

title: Add Reset to Defaults button for each concept
description: No way to restore initial state after corrupting inputs. If user removes all processes, changes all matrix values, or clears the reference string, only a page refresh recovers. Add Reset to Defaults button in each concept's control bar.
acceptanceCriteria: Each concept has a Reset to Defaults button
Button restores the initial state values defined in useOSModule
Confirmation dialog before resetting if there are unsaved results

title: Reorder concepts from domain grouping to pedagogical sequence
description: Current order: scheduling, page-replacement, deadlock, memory, mem-alloc, thread-sync. Pedagogically correct: scheduling → memory → page-replacement → mem-alloc → thread-sync → deadlock (concepts that require understanding of earlier ones come later). Add difficulty badges.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar shows concepts in pedagogical order
Each concept has a difficulty badge (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced)
Sidebar shows grouping headers (CPU, Memory, Concurrency)

title: Add color-independent encoding to all visualizations for color-blind users
description: Red/green alone distinguish page faults from hits, deadlock from safe, holding from waiting. No pattern, shape, or icon supplements exist. Add shape/icon/text backups for every color-coded state.
acceptanceCriteria: PageFrameGrid uses checkmark/X icons alongside green/red
RAG uses different line styles (solid vs double) alongside colors
ThreadSync uses icon badges alongside color for state
All visualizations are distinguishable in grayscale

title: Replace raw HTML inputs with shared UI components (Input, Select, Label)
description: OSModule uses native <select> and <input> with manual Tailwind (OSModule.tsx:2374-2384). NetworkingModule and DatabaseModule use shared @/components/ui components. When global theme changes, OS module will visually diverge.
acceptanceCriteria: All <select> elements replaced with shared Select component
All <input> elements replaced with shared Input component
All <label> elements replaced with shared Label component
Visual consistency with other modules maintained

title: Extract shared scheduling helpers to avoid duplication between scheduling.ts and mlfq-scheduler.ts
description: scheduling.ts:59-110 and mlfq-scheduler.ts:87-133 both define identical finalise() and pushGantt() helpers. Extract to a shared scheduling-utils.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: finalise() and pushGantt() exist in a single scheduling-utils.ts
Both scheduling.ts and mlfq-scheduler.ts import from shared file
No duplicate function definitions remain

title: Add Gantt chart intermediate time tick marks and axis labels
description: GanttChart (OSModule.tsx:207-212) only shows 0 and maxTime. No intermediate markers, no 'Time' axis label. For simulations longer than 20 ticks, the axis is useless. Add 4-6 evenly spaced tick marks.
acceptanceCriteria: Gantt chart shows 4-6 intermediate time markers
X-axis has 'Time' label
Tick marks scale with simulation length

title: Replace key={i} with stable IDs for all mutable lists
description: 19 instances of key={i} for mutable lists (process cards line 2408, Gantt blocks, request items). When items are deleted or reordered, React incorrectly reuses DOM nodes. Use stable IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Process cards use key={p.name} or unique ID
Alloc request items use stable key
No key={i} remains for lists that can be mutated

title: Add process lifecycle state machine visualization (New → Ready → Running → Blocked → Terminated)
description: Process lifecycle is the most fundamental OS concept (OSTEP Ch.4, MIT 6.828 Lecture 3, top-10 interview topic) but is entirely missing from the module. Add an animated state machine showing process state transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: New concept 'Process Lifecycle' added to sidebar
Interactive state machine diagram with transitions
User can trigger transitions (fork, schedule, block, wake, terminate)
Step-through mode showing state changes

title: Add I/O and disk scheduling visualization (SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK)
description: Disk scheduling is covered in every OS course (OSTEP Ch.37-44) and interview prep. 4 algorithms (SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK) with head movement visualization. No existing tool does this well.
acceptanceCriteria: New concept 'Disk Scheduling' added to sidebar
4 algorithms: SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK
Head movement visualization showing seek distance
Comparison mode showing total seek time for all algorithms
Step-through mode

title: Add condition variables to thread synchronization concept
description: Thread sync covers Mutex, Semaphore, RW Lock but omits condition variables (OSTEP Ch.30, top interview topic). Add condition variable simulation with wait/signal/broadcast showing producer-consumer pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Condition Variable added as 4th sync primitive option
Producer-consumer scenario with bounded buffer
Visual shows threads waiting on condition vs holding mutex
Step-through mode with event log

title: Add system call interface visualization (user space → kernel space transition)
description: System calls are a top-10 OS interview topic (InterviewBit) and MIT lab topic. Visualize the trap/interrupt mechanism: user process → syscall instruction → mode switch → kernel handler → return. No existing tool visualizes this.
acceptanceCriteria: New concept 'System Calls' added to sidebar or as Learn content
Animated diagram showing privilege level transition
Common syscalls listed with their flow (read, write, fork, exec)
Shows register save/restore during context switch

title: Add multi-level page table visualization for real-world memory concepts
description: Memory concept only covers single-level page tables. Multi-level page tables (used by x86-64) are standard curriculum (OSTEP Ch.20) and interview material. Add visualization showing 2-level or 3-level page table walk.
acceptanceCriteria: Multi-level page table option in memory concept
Visual shows page directory → page table → frame walk
TLB interaction with multi-level tables shown
Comparison of memory overhead: single vs multi-level

title: Add 'Predict what happens next' mode for active learning engagement
description: Research shows active prediction improves learning (Mayer's principles, OS Odyssey study). Before each step, pause and ask 'What page will be evicted?' or 'Which process runs next?' with instant feedback. No existing tool does this.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle for 'Quiz Mode' in step controls
Before each step, a prediction prompt appears
User selects from multiple choice options
Immediate feedback: correct/incorrect with explanation
Score tracking within session

title: Add copy/export simulation results as shareable image or text
description: No way to share results. Students cannot show classmates 'look what I found'. Add copy-to-clipboard for metrics table, and export Gantt/visualization as PNG. Research shows shareability drives adoption.
acceptanceCriteria: Copy button on metrics tables copies formatted text to clipboard
Export button on visualizations generates PNG via html2canvas
Exported images include a watermark/attribution

title: Add progress tracking — concepts explored, algorithms run, comparison completed
description: Zero engagement/gamification mechanics (Octalysis score 5/40). Add lightweight progress: track which concepts user has explored, which algorithms they've run, badge for completing all comparisons. Store in localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar shows checkmark for explored concepts
Progress counter: 'X/6 concepts explored'
Badge earned for running Compare All in each concept
Progress persists across page refreshes via localStorage

title: Add worst-case input challenge mode: 'Break the algorithm'
description: Never-been-done innovation: invert the relationship. Instead of 'watch algorithm work', challenge users to craft the WORST input. 'Make FCFS have the highest average wait time possible.' 'Find an input where Belady's anomaly is most extreme.' First tool to make failure the feature.
acceptanceCriteria: Challenge mode toggle available for each concept
Predefined challenges with scoring (e.g., maximize wait time, maximize page faults)
Leaderboard showing best scores
Explanation of why worst-case inputs matter in practice

title: Add algorithm sonification — hear the difference between O(n²) and O(n log n) scheduling
description: Web Audio API enables zero-latency synthesis. Each scheduling tick produces a tone whose pitch maps to the running process. Different algorithms produce different melodies. FCFS sounds monotone; MLFQ sounds dynamic. First OS visualization tool with audio.
acceptanceCriteria: Sound toggle button in scheduling controls
Each process has a distinct pitch
Scheduling ticks produce tones via Web Audio API
Speed control affects playback rate
Respects prefers-reduced-motion by defaulting to off

title: Add OS-to-Cloud bridge: map FCFS/RR/Priority to Kubernetes pod scheduling
description: 67% of K8s teams use eBPF. No tool connects OS scheduling concepts to container scheduling. Add a 'Real World' panel showing how each OS algorithm maps to Kubernetes: FCFS→FIFO queue, Priority→PriorityClass, RR→ResourceQuota fair sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: Real World section in each concept's Learn tab
CPU scheduling maps to K8s scheduler concepts
Memory management maps to container cgroups/limits
Thread sync maps to distributed locks/leader election

title: Cap simulation input sizes to prevent UI thread blocking
description: Burst values up to 999999 block the UI for seconds in SRTF tick-by-tick loop (scheduling.ts:299-316). Reference strings of 100000 items exhaust memory. Add input guards: max burst 1000, max reference string length 200, max processes 20.
acceptanceCriteria: Number inputs have max attributes enforced
Reference string is truncated with warning at 200 entries
Process count capped at 20 with Add button disabled
Error message shown when limits are hit

title: Add Buddy System memory allocator to mem-alloc concept
description: Memory allocation covers First/Best/Worst Fit but omits Buddy System (used by Linux kernel, OSTEP Ch.17, common interview topic). Add power-of-2 splitting/merging visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: Buddy System added as 4th allocation algorithm
Visual shows power-of-2 block splitting on allocation
Visual shows buddy merging on deallocation
Included in Compare All mode

title: Add concept-specific empty states with example scenarios
description: All 6 concepts show identical 'Configure and press Run' centered text. Add concept-specific empty states with: illustration, 1-sentence description of what the visualization shows, and a 'Try Example' button that loads a pre-configured interesting scenario.
acceptanceCriteria: Each concept has a unique empty state illustration
Try Example button loads a pre-configured scenario and runs it
Example scenarios are educational (e.g., deadlock cycle, Belady's case)

title: Add WHY explanations to step descriptions across all engines
description: Step descriptions say WHAT happened but not WHY. FCFS: 'P1 starts execution (burst=5)' should add 'because it arrived first (FCFS serves in arrival order)'. MLFQ demotion should explain 'penalized for CPU-bound behavior'. Deadlock release should explain safe-sequence logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Each engine's step descriptions include a WHY clause
WHY text is wrapped in a distinct style (e.g., italic or muted color)
Beginner could understand each step without external reference
At least 3 description types per engine have WHY text

title: Add deadlock cycle display using user-visible names instead of internal IDs
description: Deadlock cycle (OSModule.tsx:1720) displays internal IDs like 'p0 → p1 → p2' instead of user-visible names 'P0 → P1 → P2'. Apply name lookup from dlProcesses when rendering the cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Deadlock cycle shows user-visible process names
Safe sequence in Banker's also shows user-visible names
Properties panel deadlock info uses user-visible names
